methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $46M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $29.0B.

data.narrative:
Hypersonix Launch Systems is a Series A-stage defense and security company that has raised $46M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of four same-sector, same-stage private companies—Scale AI, Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries—alongside two public defense comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS), providing triangulation across 10 total data points.

Two methods were applied. The stage-progression approach uses the $46M in total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression, yielding $92M. The peer-median method anchors to the median valuation of four same-stage Defense & Security companies, which is $400M, with a range spanning $150M (Ares Industries) to $29.0B (Scale AI). The peer-median approach better captures sector-level arbitrage and growth expectations but exhibits material dispersion due to Scale AI's outsized position.

The triangulated valuation range is $78M (low, weighted toward stage-progression) to $460M (high, weighted toward peer-median), with a median implied value of $246M. Key uncertainties include whether Hypersonix's specific technology, addressable market, and go-to-market trajectory warrant positioning toward the peer-median or lower end of the range, and whether the $29B Scale AI outlier reflects a genuine comparable or a data quality issue that warrants exclusion.
